name: Las Tres Marías Trail
name_original: Valle de Arbás – trail running circular desde Casares de Arbás (Reserva de la Biosfera Alto Bernesga)
description: Challenge yourself on the difficult 7.0-mile Las Tres Marías Trail, a jogging route with steep ascents, dense forests, and rewarding views.
region_intro: Jogging around Villamanín offers a diverse landscape for runners, characterized by its position within the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve. The region features a mix of dense forests, such as the Ciñera Beech Forest, and more open mountain terrain with notable peaks and passes. Running routes often traverse varied elevations, providing both challenging ascents and more gentle paths through natural environments. Best jogging routes around Villamanín The most popular jogging route is Faedo de Ciñera – trail running circular (Reserva de la Biosfera Alto Bernesga), a 4.0 miles (6.4 km) trail that takes 46 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores a scenic beech forest within a biosphere reserve. Another top favourite among local runners is Pico Gallo y Monte Brición – trail running circular desde Cármenes (Reserva de la Biosfera Los Argüellos), a difficult 6.9 miles (11.1 km) path. This route offers a challenging run through mountainous terrain with significant elevation changes. Local runners also love the Las Tres Marías Trail, a 7.0 miles (11.2 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 33 minutes. Jogging around Villamanín is defined by its beech forests, mountain passes, and biosphere reserves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate forest paths to challenging mountain running routes. The routes in Villamanín are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from 3 reviews. More than 160 runners have used komoot to explore Villamanín's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Villamanín?
A: Villamanín offers a diverse selection of over 20 running routes. These paths vary in difficulty, ranging from moderate forest trails to challenging mountain runs, ensuring options for different fitness levels and preferences.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging paths in Villamanín?
A: Yes, Villamanín has options suitable for beginners. While many routes are moderate to difficult, you can find gentler paths. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052003964/faedo-de-cinera-trail-running-circular-reserva-de-la-biosfera-alto-bernesga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Faedo de Ciñera – trail running circular&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4-mile (6.4 km) route through a scenic beech forest, offering a good introduction to the area's terrain.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the jogging routes around Villamanín?
A: The running routes in Villamanín are known for their varied and beautiful scenery. You'll encounter dense beech forests, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5235085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciñera Beech Forest&lt;/a&gt;, open mountain terrain with notable peaks, and paths within the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve. Many routes offer panoramic views and traverse natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4073598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Calderones Gorge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Villamanín?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Villamanín are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052003964/faedo-de-cinera-trail-running-circular-reserva-de-la-biosfera-alto-bernesga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Faedo de Ciñera – trail running circular&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through a beautiful forest loop. Another challenging circular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056018377/pico-gallo-y-monte-bricion-trail-running-circular-desde-carmenes-reserva-de-la-biosfera-los-argueellos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Gallo y Monte Brición – trail running circular desde Cármenes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some challenging running routes for experienced runners in Villamanín?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, Villamanín offers several difficult routes with significant elevation changes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056018377/pico-gallo-y-monte-bricion-trail-running-circular-desde-carmenes-reserva-de-la-biosfera-los-argueellos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Gallo y Monte Brición – trail running circular desde Cármenes&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 6.9-mile (11.1 km) path through mountainous terrain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055968994/las-tres-marias-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Tres Marías Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 7.0-mile (11.2 km) route with considerable ascent.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Villamanín?
A: The running routes in Villamanín are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, from serene beech forests to challenging mountain passes, and the opportunity to explore the natural beauty within the biosphere reserves. The variety of routes caters to different abilities, making it a popular destination for trail running.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the running routes?
A: Yes, many running routes in Villamanín pass by or offer views of significant landmarks. You can explore the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5235085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciñera Beech Forest&lt;/a&gt;, discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4073598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Calderones Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, or even catch glimpses of mountain peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5556607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Fontañán Summit&lt;/a&gt;. The region is rich in natural beauty and cultural heritage.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Villamanín?
A: The best time for jogging in Villamanín largely depends on your preference. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with autumn showcasing stunning fall foliage in the beech forests. Summer is also popular, though some higher elevation routes might be warmer. Winter can be beautiful for running, especially on lower trails, but be prepared for colder conditions and potential snow at higher altitudes.

Q: Are there any less crowded running trails in Villamanín?
A: While popular routes like Faedo de Ciñera can see more visitors, Villamanín's extensive network of over 20 trails means you can often find quieter paths. Exploring some of the longer or more challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38148415" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pola de Gordón Town Center – La Muria mountain pass loop&lt;/a&gt;, can lead you to more secluded areas, especially during off-peak times or weekdays.

Q: Can I find running routes that are suitable for families in Villamanín?
A: For families looking to jog together, routes with moderate difficulty and shorter distances are ideal. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052003964/faedo-de-cinera-trail-running-circular-reserva-de-la-biosfera-alto-bernesga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Faedo de Ciñera – trail running circular&lt;/a&gt; is a good option, being a moderate 4-mile (6.4 km) path through a beautiful forest that is generally accessible for active families. Always check the route details for elevation and terrain to ensure it suits your family's capabilities.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer views of mountain passes?
A: Absolutely. Villamanín is situated in a mountainous region, and many running routes offer stunning views of and sometimes traverse mountain passes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38148415" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pola de Gordón Town Center – La Muria mountain pass loop&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route that includes a significant mountain pass. You can also find views towards &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6333018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cubilla Pass&lt;/a&gt; from various points in the region.

Q: What is the typical duration for a jogging route in Villamanín?
A: The duration of jogging routes in Villamanín varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. Shorter, moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052003964/faedo-de-cinera-trail-running-circular-reserva-de-la-biosfera-alto-bernesga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Faedo de Ciñera – trail running circular&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in under an hour (around 46 minutes). More challenging and longer routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38148415" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pola de Gordón Town Center – La Muria mountain pass loop&lt;/a&gt;, can take over 3 hours to complete.
tips: Fantastic views of the 3 Marías and on the other side, the Casares reservoir. An authentic viewpoint in the middle of the road.
We are in the Collada Gistreo, located at an altitude of 1634 meters, from where we have perfect views of the Tres Marías and the entire valley.
Picturesque path under the shade of a dense pine forest. Passage area of the Casares de Arbás route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Las Tres Marías Trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is notably challenging, featuring dense forests, steep ascents, and river crossings. You can expect muddy and slippery conditions, with some sections so steep that you might find yourself grabbing onto roots. The ground can be soft with intertwining branches and roots, and some parts are narrow with drop-offs. Descending also presents challenges, often involving navigating rocks, roots, and ankle-deep river water.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for jogging, given its difficulty?
faqs.answer: While listed for jogging, the Las Tres Marías Trail is described as a difficult trekking and climbing route. It features sustained steep sections and challenging terrain. Experienced trail runners looking for a significant physical challenge might attempt it, but beginners or those expecting a typical running path should be aware of its arduous nature. It's more suited for fast hiking or very technical trail running.

faqs.question: Where does the Las Tres Marías Trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting from Casares de Arbás. Parking is typically available in or near the village, which serves as the trailhead. Specific parking areas are usually well-marked for visitors accessing the trail.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers beautiful and enchanting scenery, showcasing a pure, virgin forest. Key highlights include the View of Las Tres Marías, the Collada Gistreo (1,634 m), and the Pine Forest Near Collado de Gistreo. You'll also encounter multiple waterfalls and enjoy breathtaking views of lowland communities and distant islands from the peaks.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route fully encompasses the Sendero de las Tres Marías. It also passes through significant sections of the Transcantábrica trail (covering about 48% of its length) and a small part of the Puente del Glcaciar trail (about 4%).

faqs.question: Is the Las Tres Marías Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Given its location within the Reserva de la Biosfera del Alto Bernesga, dogs are generally permitted. However, due to the trail's difficult and rugged terrain, including steep sections and potential river crossings, it might be very challenging for some dogs. Always keep your dog on a leash and ensure they are fit enough for such a strenuous route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail's conditions can vary significantly with seasons. While the research doesn't specify an ideal time, considering the muddy and slippery conditions mentioned, spring and autumn might offer pleasant temperatures but could also bring more rain. Summer would likely be drier but potentially hotter. Always check local weather conditions before you go, especially for a trail of this difficulty.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The Las Tres Marías Trail is located within the Reserva de la Biosfera del Alto Bernesga. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking or jogging on established trails within Spanish Biosphere Reserves. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning wildlife and environmental protection.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a challenging route like this?
faqs.answer: For this difficult trail, even if attempting to jog, you should bring sturdy trail shoes with good grip, plenty of water, snacks, and appropriate layers of clothing. Trekking poles can be very helpful for stability on steep and slippery sections. A first-aid kit, navigation tools (like the komoot app), and a fully charged phone are also essential.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities near Casares de Arbás after completing the trail?
faqs.answer: Casares de Arbás is a small village. While specific amenities like cafes or pubs aren't detailed, it's common for such villages in rural areas to have at least one local establishment where you can find refreshments or a meal. Accommodation options might also be available, but it's advisable to check in advance, especially during peak seasons.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife or plants can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through a pure, virgin forest with thick vegetation, offering opportunities to observe abundant wildlife. You might encounter various bird species and other forest animals. The unique flora includes mossy trees and pitcher plants, especially near the summits.

waypoints.name: Pine Forest Near Collado de Gistreo
